summary: Follow-up automation and hygiene gaps found while reconciling llm_wiki_vnext_detailed_code_review_report_ko_20260419.md against the current repository state.

observations.problem: external_report_reference_issues only discovers .pdf and .docx files under external-reports even though current corpus Source trace sections reference external-reports/위키_콘텐츠_종합_검토_보고서.md.
observations.why_it_matters: A markdown external report can become a hidden corpus source dependency outside the raw registry/source-of-truth contract while lint and eval still pass if the local file happens to exist.
observations.automation_candidate: Make external report extensions policy-backed or include .md by default, then add corpus/documentation regression tests that require corpus source traces to use raw-backed source-of-truth paths or explicit reviewed exemptions.
observations.suggested_followup: The external report audit now reads policy-backed extensions and the live policy includes .md alongside .pdf and .docx. Keep future external report formats behind doc_audit.external_report_extensions and decide separately whether markdown external reports should be promoted into raw registry entries or allowed only as documentation-surface references.

observations.problem: An editable install leaves llm_wiki_vnext.egg-info/ in the repo root, and wiki_manifest currently includes that generated package metadata in manifest inventory.
observations.why_it_matters: Release smoke and manifest comparison can normalize a locally contaminated inventory, making generated build metadata look like intentional source surface.
observations.automation_candidate: Exclude *.egg-info/, build/, and dist/ from manifest and release archive inventory, mirror the policy in .gitignore/public surface tests, and add a release-smoke regression fixture.
observations.suggested_followup: Manifest and release archive inventory now exclude *.egg-info/, build/, and dist/, and root hygiene docs plus .gitignore mirror that generated-artifact boundary. Keep future packaging metadata treated like .venv, tmp, cache, and coverage artifacts.

observations.problem: Current full-vault gates can return success while raw_registry_preflight and wiki_lint still emit warning debt, so release/readiness language depends on an implicit operator interpretation of warnings.
observations.why_it_matters: The repository can be technically passing while accumulating unregistered raw files, raw markdown metadata debt, or pending frontmatter migration debt that should be visible as a stricter readiness tier.
observations.automation_candidate: Add an optional strict gate or warning-budget target that fails on selected warning types or nonzero warning counts without changing the normal development loop.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated via ops/scripts/warning_budget.py, ops/schemas/warning-budget-report.schema.json, policy strict_warning_budget.release_clean, make warning-budget, make check-strict, and make release-clean. Keep the zero-budget set focused on warning debt that must block strict release readiness.
